data.title: Diana Shipping announces time charter contract for m/v Myrsini with Cargill
data.description:
• Diana Shipping (NYSE:DSX) on Wednesday announced that through a separate wholly-owned subsidiary, it has entered into a time charter contract with Cargill International, for one of its Kamsarmax dry bulk vessels, the m/v Myrsini.
  • The gross charter rate is $13,000 per day, minus a 4.75% commission paid to third parties, for a period until minimum January 1, 2026, up to maximum February 28, 2026.
  • The charter is expected to commence on February 28, 2025.
  • The employment of “Myrsini” is anticipated to generate about $3.95 million of gross revenue for the minimum scheduled period of the time charter, the company said.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Travelers Companies upgraded to Outperform at KBW on reserve release prospects
data.description:
Keefe, Bruyette & Woods upgraded The Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV) to Outperform from Market Perform, citing the likelihood that the insurer overstated its 2024 GAAP loss and allocated loss adjusted expenses ("ALAE") reserves by ~$1.39B.

Travelers (NYSE:TRV) stock rose 1.2% in Wednesday morning trading.

KBW's analysis of the company's year-end 2024 GAAP reserve estimates that Travelers' (TRV) GAAP loss and ALAE were overstated, mostly on workers' compensation, personal lines, and fidelity and surety redundancies that outpaced general liability, especially in its bond & specialty segment.

"Following TRV’s considerable general liability reserve strengthening, our primary concern was that its significant workers' compensation reserve releases would slow," KBW analyst Meyer Shields wrote in a note to clients. "Directionally, that still seems reasonable, but we’re more optimistic about the size of the current 'cushion'."

KBW raised its estimate for 2025 reserve releases to $392M from $315M and for 2026 releases to $292M from $191M. That boosts its EPS estimates for 2025 to $19.30 from $19.05 and for 2026 to $24.90 from $24.60.

Shields' Outperform rating on Travelers (TRV) contrasts with the SA Quant rating and the average Wall Street rating, both at Hold.

data.title: Parsons projects $7B-$7.5B revenue for 2025 amid strong infrastructure and federal growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Carey Smith highlighted record results for fiscal 2024, including $6.7 billion in revenue, 22% organic revenue growth, and adjusted EBITDA growth of 30%. The company achieved a 50 basis point expansion in adjusted EBITDA margin and record contract awards of $7 billion, driven by a 71% win rate.
  • Smith emphasized strategic acquisitions, including BlackSignal and BCC Engineering, which enhanced capabilities in offensive cyber operations, electronic warfare, and critical infrastructure engineering. The TRS Group acquisition will bolster environmental remediation capabilities, particularly for PFAS solutions.
  • CFO Matt Ofilos noted that the company achieved $1.7 billion in Q4 revenue, a 16% increase year-over-year, and $147 million in adjusted EBITDA. Adjusted EBITDA margin was impacted by $29 million in program adjustments but would have been 10% excluding these items.
  • Federal Solutions revenue grew 19% year-over-year in Q4, driven by recent contract wins. Critical Infrastructure revenue rose 12%, supported by organic growth in North America and the Middle East.

Outlook

  • Parsons expects 2025 revenue between $7.0 billion and $7.5 billion, representing 7.5% growth at the midpoint, with 5% organic growth. Adjusted EBITDA is projected between $640 million and $710 million, reflecting 12% growth and 30 basis points of margin expansion.
  • Cash flow from operating activities is forecasted at $420 million to $480 million, with a free cash flow conversion rate of approximately 86% of adjusted net income.
  • Management cited growth opportunities in artificial intelligence, cyber, and critical infrastructure segments, as well as continued benefits from global infrastructure spending and alignment with federal priorities.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenue was $1.7 billion compared to analysts' estimate of $1.76 billion. Adjusted EPS was not explicitly stated in the transcript.
  • Organic revenue growth for the year reached 22%, with double-digit growth in all business units. The company achieved $7 billion in record contract awards for the year, with a trailing 12-month book-to-bill ratio of 1.0x.
  • Federal Solutions achieved an annual revenue increase of 33%, with growth in critical infrastructure protection and cyber markets. Adjusted EBITDA for this segment rose 43%, with a margin of 10.4%.
  • Critical Infrastructure revenue grew 13% for the year, primarily organically. Adjusted EBITDA increased by 8%, with a margin of 6.9% impacted by adjustments related to legacy contracts.

Q&A

  • Tobey Sommer, Truist Securities: Asked for updates on confidential customer work. Carey Smith explained that the option year two of the related contract is funded at $242 million through February 2026, with potential for increased volume if related programs restart.
  • Louie DiPalma, William Blair: Queried the confidential contract’s revenue assumptions. Management confirmed $242 million is included in the 2025 plan but noted uncertainties around the unfunded portion.
  • Andrew Wittmann, Baird: Inquired about Critical Infrastructure margins. Ofilos clarified that adjusted margins would reach 10.2% excluding adjustments and highlighted a path to further expansion.
  • Sheila Kahyaoglu, Jefferies: Asked about broader 2025 guidance drivers. Management attributed growth to double-digit expansion in most of the portfolio, despite anticipated lower volumes on certain transitioning programs.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on uncertainties surrounding confidential contracts and procurement delays. Questions emphasized clarity on contract funding and margin expansion.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, citing strong performance across segments and robust alignment with market drivers. Phrases such as "we are very pleased" and "we are confident" were frequent.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained positive but slightly more defensive regarding uncertainties in confidential government work.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance for 2025 represents a shift from the record organic growth of over 20% achieved in 2024 to mid-single-digit expectations.
  • Federal Solutions continued its robust growth trajectory, while Critical Infrastructure margins showed improvement, excluding legacy contract impacts.
  • Analysts maintained a cautious stance on confidential contracts and margin sustainability, slightly intensifying their scrutiny compared to Q3.
  • Management's confidence in operational execution and strategic alignment remained consistent, supported by strong win rates and backlog.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted a reduced volume in confidential contracts due to related program pauses, with long-term continuation contingent on external factors.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the pace of federal procurement, potential funding shifts, and the impact of transitioning programs on 2025 growth.
  • Legacy contract adjustments, although completed, were noted as a one-time impact, with future risks centered on execution and macroeconomic factors.

Final Takeaway

Parsons delivered record financial and operational results for 2024, driven by strong organic growth and strategic acquisitions. The company projects continued momentum in 2025, with revenue guidance of $7.0 billion to $7.5 billion and a focus on expanding margins and cash flow. While uncertainties surrounding confidential contracts and procurement cadence remain, management's confidence in its diversified portfolio and alignment with market priorities underscores its growth trajectory.

data.title: La-Z-Boy targets 8.5%-9.5% Q4 operating margin with new store openings and strategic expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: La-Z-Boy Incorporated (NYSE:LZB) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Melinda Whittington stated that La-Z-Boy achieved consolidated delivered sales of $522 million, a 4% increase year-over-year, supported by sales growth across all segments, with the retail segment showing an 11% increase. Same-store sales also grew by 7%, driven by improved conversion rates, higher average tickets, and design sales.
  • The company highlighted its Century Vision growth strategy, which includes expanding its La-Z-Boy Furniture Galleries network to over 400 stores in the coming years, with three new stores opened in Q3 and agreements signed to acquire four additional stores in Ohio and Michigan.
  • CEO Whittington mentioned that La-Z-Boy's marketing campaigns and superior in-store execution continue to resonate with consumers, resulting in market share gains despite a challenging macro environment.
  • CFO Taylor Luebke reported non-GAAP operating income of $35 million, with a 20-basis-point year-over-year increase in operating margin due to lower input costs and improved sourcing.
  • The Joybird business delivered a 10% increase in written sales and achieved breakeven profitability, attributed to improved execution and favorable product mix.

Outlook

  • Management projects Q4 delivered sales in the range of $545 million to $565 million and non-GAAP operating margins between 8.5% and 9.5%.
  • Five to seven new La-Z-Boy Furniture Galleries are expected to open in Q4, contributing to the company's retail network expansion.
  • CFO Luebke noted that the company has plans to mitigate potential tariff impacts through its global supply chain and pricing strategies.
  • The company remains optimistic about outperforming the market while maintaining investments in its Century Vision strategy.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated non-GAAP gross margin increased by 160 basis points year-over-year, supported by a favorable shift in sales mix toward the retail segment, lower input costs, and improved sourcing.
  • Cash flow from operations reached $57 million during the quarter, an 18% increase compared to the prior year. Year-to-date cash flow totaled $125 million, up 19% year-over-year.
  • The company returned $20 million to shareholders during Q3 through dividends and share repurchases, with $90 million returned year-to-date.
  • Joybird achieved breakeven profitability in Q3, with delivered sales growing 9% year-over-year.

Q&A

  • Taylor Zick, KeyBanc Capital: Asked about sales performance trends and Q4 expectations. CEO Whittington noted that sales were strongest during the holiday period in November, with positive trends across all months of Q3. February was described as early in the quarter, with choppy traffic observed during President’s Day.
  • Bobby Griffin, Raymond James: Inquired about the progress of wholesale margins and international business disruptions. CFO Luebke mentioned that North American wholesale margins have improved for three consecutive quarters, while the DFS partnership in the UK remains a longer-term growth opportunity.
  • Anthony Lebiedzinski, Sidoti & Company: Requested details on regional sales trends and Q3 guidance. CEO Whittington indicated no significant regional variations in sales, while CFO Luebke explained that Q3 margins are seasonally lower due to holiday downtime and international business challenges.
  • Brad Thomas, KeyBanc: Asked about potential tariff impacts. CEO Whittington highlighted La-Z-Boy’s North American manufacturing footprint as a competitive advantage, with plans to mitigate tariff-related costs through pricing and supply chain adjustments.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on margin sustainability, international business challenges, and consumer demand trends. Questions reflected concerns about macro headwinds and potential tariff impacts.
  • Management’s tone was confident during prepared remarks, emphasizing strong execution and strategic progress. However, responses during the Q&A revealed a more cautious approach to addressing near-term challenges.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a consistent tone, reiterating confidence in its Century Vision strategy. Analysts remained focused on macroeconomic headwinds and operational pressures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 revenue of $522 million represented a slight increase from Q2’s $521 million, driven by stronger retail and Joybird performance.
  • Operating margin expanded by 20 basis points in Q3, compared to a decline in Q2 due to challenges in the casegoods and international businesses.
  • Written same-store sales growth of 7% in Q3 marked a significant improvement over Q2’s 1% decline.
  • Analysts continued to focus on wholesale margins, international disruptions, and the trajectory of Joybird’s profitability, which showed notable improvement in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing macroeconomic challenges, including elevated mortgage rates, housing affordability issues, and a volatile tariff environment.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of margin improvements, particularly within the international wholesale business.
  • CFO Luebke emphasized the company’s plans to address tariff uncertainties through agile supply chain management and pricing actions.

Final Takeaway

La-Z-Boy demonstrated strong execution in Q3 2025, achieving sales growth across all segments and improving margins despite industry headwinds. The company’s Century Vision strategy continues to drive progress, with expanded retail operations and market share gains. While challenges in the international wholesale business and macroeconomic uncertainties persist, La-Z-Boy remains focused on its long-term growth objectives, supported by disciplined financial management and strategic investments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Least shorted S&P 500 stocks in January
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Walmart (WMT) and Philip Morris International (PM) were among the least shorted S&P 500 (SP500) stocks at January end.

As of Jan. 31, Alphabet Class C (GOOG) had a short interest of 43.98M shares, representing 0.36% of its shares outstanding. The tech firm saw its volume of shorted shares increase 4.8% during the month compared to Dec-end. Alphabet Class A shares (GOOGL) had a short interest of 0.49% of float.

Walmart (PM) followed next, with 36.29M shares sold short at Jan-end, representing 0.45% of the float.

Philip Morris International (PM) took the third spot with a short interest of 7.16M shares at month end, representing 0.46% of its float. This marks a ~22.2% decrease in short interest from the previous month.

Here are the top 20 least shorted S&P 500 stocks as a percentage of float, as of January:

  • Alphabet Inc C (GOOG) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.36%

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow inch lower, day after S&P 500 touches record high
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages inched lower on Wednesday, a day after the S&P 500 notched up a record high, while investors assessed more tariff plans of U.S. President Donald Trump.

Early on and the S&P 500(SP500) was -0.2%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was near even at 4.54%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) dipped 2 basis points to 4.28%.

The S&P 500 finished at a closing high Tuesday after investors spent a choppy session with their attention on geopolitics and potential deal-making.

"Talks over Ukraine were an important catalyst for yesterday’s advance as hopes for an end to the conflict helped power the European advance, where equities have seen a clear outperformance against their global counterparts so far this year," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Trump's trade policies continued to dominate the headlines, as he said he would probably announce 25% tariffs on imports of cars, semiconductors, and pharmaceuticals on April 2.

"Trump rapidly retreated from trade taxes that would be very visible to US consumers—Gen Z would notice tariff-induced inflation for avocado on toast. Less visible taxes (like aluminum) have endured," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The new proposals are partially visible—taxing new cars raises second-hand car prices and the cost of car insurance—but probably sufficiently disguised as to prevent a repeat retreat, Donovan added.

Housing starts dropped more than forecast in January, while building permits unexpectedly ticked up.

On the economic side, the Atlanta Fed's business inflation expectations report is due on Wednesday. Furthermore, markets will also recieve the FOMC meeting minutes later in the day.

data.title: Qiagen, Bio-Techne downgraded at Baird amid NIH funding concerns
data.description:
• Baird has downgraded Qiagen (NYSE:QGEN) and Bio-Techne (NASDAQ:TECH) from overweight to neutral, citing concerns over NIH funding and the amount of revenue the companies receive from that agency.
  • The investment bank cut its price target for Qiagen to $42 from $52 (~5% upside based on Feb. 18 close), and $68 from $88 for Bio-Techne (~4% upside).
  • On Feb. 7, the NIH said it would cap coverage of so-called indirect costs at 15% for new and existing research grants. These costs include research infrastructure and operating expenses such laboratory construction and maintenance, facilities, equipment, and computing. Several days later, a judge issued a temporary restraining order preventing the cap from going into effect.
  • Analyst Catherine Schulte said that based on a survey of academic researchers Baird conducted on the potential impact of the indirect cost cap, responses included "we would be out of business in a year" and "will be catastrophic."
  • She added that ~5%-6% of each companies' revenue comes from the NIH.

data.title: FuboTV falls on report DOJ looking at deal with Disney's Hulu+
data.description:
FuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) dropped 3.2% amid a report that the Dept. of Justice is looking at Disney's (NYSE:DIS) plans to combine its Hulu + Live TV business with rival Fubo.

DOJ attorneys are questioning media players if the combination may lead to Disney (NYSE:DIS) raising programming costs for pay-TV distributors, according to traders, who cited a Capitol Forum report that circulated on Wednesday.

Last month, FuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) announced it would merge Disney's Hulu + Live TV business with Fubo. As part of the deal, Fubo agreed to settle all litigation with Disney and ESPN related to Venu Sports.

Disney (DIS), Fox (FOXA), and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) disbanded their three-way joint venture, Venu Sports, in the wake of the settlement with FUBO.

data.title: Perion Network outlines 2025 revenue target of $400M-$420M with Perion One strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Perion Network Ltd. (NASDAQ:PERI) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Tal Jacobson announced the launch of the Perion One strategy, aiming to unify all technologies under a single platform to simplify and optimize omnichannel advertising. This strategy focuses on AI-driven solutions for personalized messaging and operational efficiency.
  • The Perion One platform will integrate multiple advertising channels, including CTV, digital out of home, retail media, and social, under one system. The rollout is scheduled for the coming months, with expectations to streamline processes and improve campaign performance.
  • Jacobson welcomed new senior hires, including Stephen Yap as Chief Revenue Officer, Kenny Lau as Chief Product Officer, and Mina Naguib as Chief Technology Officer, emphasizing their roles in advancing the company’s transformation and growth strategies.
  • CFO Elad Tzubery highlighted the completion of a $46.9 million share repurchase program and stated that operational cost reductions in 2025 are expected to improve profit margins.

Outlook

  • Management provided 2025 guidance, projecting annual revenue between $400 million and $420 million, adjusted EBITDA of $40 million to $42 million, and adjusted EBITDA to contribution ex-TAC margin of 22%.
  • The company expects to achieve operational efficiencies from the Perion One strategy, with the full impact anticipated in 2026 but partial benefits reflected in the second half of 2025.
  • Search advertising is expected to remain stable, contributing approximately 20% of revenue in 2025, despite the non-renewal of the Microsoft Bing contract.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenue totaled $129.6 million compared to $234.2 million in Q4 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $15.5 million, representing a 12% adjusted EBITDA margin.
  • Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share for Q4 2024 were $0.33 vs. analysts' estimates of $0.32. Revenue was $129.6 million vs. analysts' estimates of $130.4 million.
  • Digital out of home revenue grew 57% year-over-year in Q4, accounting for 27% of advertising solutions revenue. CTV revenue increased by 10%, representing 15% of advertising solutions revenue, while retail media revenue rose 34% to $27 million.
  • Full-year 2024 revenue was $498.3 million, a decrease of 33% year-over-year, attributed to declining search revenue and weaker performance in open web video and standard ad formats. However, growth engines like CTV, digital out of home, and retail media outpaced market growth.

Q&A

  • Andrew Marok, Raymond James: Asked about the open web business and its recovery potential within the Perion One platform. CEO Jacobson stated that while open web remains challenging, the company is optimistic about opportunities as new advertisers onboard the platform.
  • Jason Helfstein, Oppenheimer: Inquired about the impact of automation on gross margins and sales efficiency. Jacobson explained that AI-driven automation reduces manual tasks, enhancing efficiency, and consolidating marketing efforts under a single brand increases ROI.
  • Eric Martinuzzi, Lake Street: Questioned the deceleration of CTV growth in Q4. CFO Tzubery attributed it to budget shifts toward digital out of home and seasonal factors but reaffirmed confidence in annual market outperformance.
  • Laura Martin, Needham & Co.: Asked about the retail media vertical and SMB potential. Jacobson noted significant adoption in physical retail stores and plans to expand SMB offerings in the future.
  • Jeff Martin, ROTH Capital: Queried about capital allocation and M&A strategy. Jacobson emphasized a focus on acquisitions that integrate into the Perion One platform, avoiding standalone operations.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, with several questions focused on the challenges in the open web and the potential of Perion One to drive growth. Concerns about revenue deceleration in CTV and the impact of the Microsoft Bing exit were evident.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone during prepared remarks and Q&A, highlighting operational efficiencies and strategic initiatives. CEO Jacobson emphasized the transformational potential of the Perion One strategy and the focus on high-margin growth areas.
  • Compared to Q3, management showed increased confidence in mitigating headwinds and leveraging the unified platform for future growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize operational efficiencies and transformative growth via the Perion One strategy, contrasting with the previous quarter's focus on addressing declining revenue in traditional formats.
  • Management tone was more optimistic, reflecting confidence in the new strategy and leadership team. Analysts' tone remained cautious, with recurring concerns about open web challenges and search revenue stability.
  • Key metrics showed improvement in digital out of home and retail media growth, while CTV growth decelerated compared to Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing challenges in the open web channel and declining search revenue due to the non-renewal of the Microsoft Bing contract.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the scalability and execution of the Perion One platform, as well as the potential for further deceleration in CTV and open web revenue.
  • Mitigation strategies outlined include operational cost reductions, a focus on high-margin growth channels, and leveraging AI-driven efficiencies.

Final Takeaway

Perion Network’s Q4 2024 earnings call highlighted a transformative shift with the introduction of the Perion One platform, which aims to unify advertising technologies and enhance operational efficiencies. Despite headwinds in search and open web revenue, strong growth in digital out of home and retail media positions the company for future expansion. Management’s 2025 guidance reflects confidence in the new strategy, supported by leadership changes and AI-driven innovations. Investors will closely monitor the execution of the Perion One rollout and its impact on revenue stabilization and growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Celanese’s stock plunges on quarterly loss, weak outlook
data.description:
Celanese’s (NYSE:CE) stock dropped as much as 23% on Wednesday, a day after the maker of specialty materials and chemicals reported a quarterly loss. The company is grappling with weak demand from the automotive, paints and industrial sectors that is expected to last into 2025.

“It appears Celanese (NYSE:CE) is still reducing capacity to match current demand by idling capacity/drawing down inventories, which is likely the main headwind to first-quarter guidance,” Arun Viswanathan, analyst at RBC Capital Markets, said in a February 18 report after Celanese (CE) reported results. “As such, we think earnings could be pressured in the first half of 2025 given overcapacity in autos and potential exposure to weaker regions.”

A key issue for investors will be identifying a catalyst to help improve the value of the shares, another analyst said.

“Absent a significant macro industrial recovery, asset divestiture is the key that could unlock a step-change in the leverage profile but with internal processes likely on-going we expect little incremental on this front,” Eric Boyes, analyst at financial-services firm Evercore ISI, said in a note to clients.

Losses on lower revenue

For the three-month period ended in December, Celanese (CE) swung to a loss of $1.91 billion from a profit of $701 million a year earlier.

Revenue slumped 8% to $2.37 billion, in line with the average estimate among analysts surveyed by S&P Global Intelligence. Celanese (CE) blamed the drop on declines product prices and sales volume, along with foreign currency moves.

The automotive and industrial sectors cut inventory in engineered materials, hurting fourth-quarter financials, according to Celanese (CE). The company lowered expenses and adjusted its output to match weaker demand, which pressured its working capital.

Weakness in demand and pricing are expected to continue into 2025, management said said. Celanese (CE) forecast earnings in the first quarter to be $0.25 to $0.50 a share amid soft demand. Second-quarter earnings are estimated at about $1 a share higher than the first quarter, according to the company.

Celanese (CE) plans to close its Luxembourg Mylar Specialty Films manufacturing site, which it co-owns with Teijin, to reduce expenses.

"With little indication of near-term recovery, it is our job to drive productivity and earnings growth at Celanese even if fundamental demand remains flat or declines further," Scott Richardson, chief executive since January, said in a statement.
